analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The strongest technical setup on the board, in the group the tape just rewarded — a new-high cybersecurity leader with TradingView STRONG BUY confirmation.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Fortinet is a rare security vendor that is both high-growth and durably profitable, pairing FortiGate firewall/appliance hardware with a faster-growing, higher-margin security-services and subscription stream (FortiGuard). Billings and deferred revenue are the key forward tells; operating margins are among the best in security software and free-cash-flow conversion is strong with a net-cash balance sheet.
highlights: Revenue in the ~$6-7B range, high-single/low-double-digit YoY growth
Service/subscription now the majority of the mix — recurring, high-margin
GAAP operating margin in the mid-20s%, non-GAAP well above
Robust FCF funding buybacks; net-cash balance sheet
Billings growth the metric to watch as the forward demand signal

accomplishments: Established leadership in unified SASE and secure networking (firewall + SD-WAN + cloud)
Sustained one of the best margin profiles in the security-software group
Expanded OT/operational-technology security, a fast-growing adjacency

journey_52w.summary: A powerful re-rating over the past year — the stock roughly doubled (+112% over three months alone) as the firewall-refresh cycle reaccelerated and the SASE story took hold, carrying it to fresh 52-week highs. It closed the latest session strong as cybersecurity names surged market-wide.

milestones.label: Late 2025
milestones.note: Firewall refresh cycle reaccelerates; billings inflect higher

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: SASE/unified-security momentum drives guidance higher

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to a new 52-week high, RSI a controlled 66 — un-extended leadership as the cyber group leads the tape

risks: Earnings in ~16 days — a high-multiple name reacts hard to any billings miss
Product (appliance) demand is lumpier than services
Enterprise-tech-spend guilt-by-association if the group turns
watch: RSI staying below overbought on the breakout
critical.valuation: Premium multiple justified by best-in-class margins + reaccelerating growth; the risk is the multiple itself into earnings. TradingView STRONG BUY corroborates the technical strength.

analyses.name: Northern Trust Corporation
analyses.verdict: A clean new-high VCP breakout in the financials group the cool-inflation, strong-bank-earnings tape just ignited. TradingView STRONG BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Northern Trust is a custody, asset-servicing and wealth-management franchise whose revenue splits between fee income (custody/administration/asset management, which scales with market levels and AUC/A) and net interest income (which benefits from higher-for-longer rates). Capital-return via buybacks and a steady dividend is a core part of the story.
highlights: Trust, investment & other servicing fees the largest revenue line
Net interest income a tailwind while rates stay elevated
Assets under custody/administration in the multi-trillions
Strong regulatory capital ratios supporting buybacks
Efficiency-ratio improvement the key operating lever

accomplishments: One of the largest global custodians by assets under custody/administration
Consistent capital return through buybacks and dividend growth
Resilient fee franchise across market cycles

journey_52w.summary: A steady climb to fresh 52-week highs, accelerating into the latest session as cooler inflation data and solid bank earnings lifted the whole financials complex. The stock cleared a tight 3-contraction VCP with its relative-strength line at the highs.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: Rising rates and market levels lift both NII and fee income

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Tightens into a VCP base near highs

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: New-high breakout, RS at highs, as bank earnings and cool inflation power financials

risks: Q2 earnings in ~8 days — a two-way catalyst
Fee income falls if equity/bond markets sell off
A sharp rate-cut cycle would compress NII
RSI 71 warm
catalysts: Market levels lifting AUC/A and fees
critical.valuation: Trades at a premium to trust-bank peers on franchise quality; TradingView STRONG BUY confirms the technical leadership.

analyses.name: Dell Technologies Inc.
analyses.verdict: A fully-digested base near highs after a +149% run — the AI-server backlog is the engine, and RSI 57 says it isn't extended. TradingView STRONG BUY.

financials.summary: Dell runs two engines: ISG (Infrastructure Solutions Group — servers/storage/networking, now supercharged by AI-optimized servers) and CSG (Client Solutions — PCs). The AI-server backlog and shipments are the swing factor; ISG operating margins are thinner than pure-play compute but volume and mix are inflecting. Strong free cash flow funds a growing dividend and buybacks.
highlights: AI-server backlog/shipments the dominant growth driver in ISG
Revenue in the ~$95-100B+ range with ISG reaccelerating
CSG (PCs) a steadier, cash-generative base
Free cash flow funding dividend growth + buybacks
ISG margin trajectory the key profitability watch

accomplishments: Emerged as a primary enterprise on-ramp for AI-server deployments
Multi-billion-dollar AI-server backlog build
Reinstated and grew capital return post-VMware spin

journey_52w.summary: A +149% three-month surge on AI-server demand carried Dell to new-high territory, yet the stock has spent recent weeks basing in a 5-contraction VCP — RSI cooled to 57 even as price held within 1.8% of its high, a textbook digestion of a big move with RS at the highs.

milestones.label: Late 2025
milestones.note: AI-server backlog builds; ISG revenue reaccelerates

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Guidance raised on AI-server momentum; stock surges

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Bases tightly near highs, RSI 57 — move fully digested

risks: ISG/AI-server margins thinner than pure-play compute
A broad AI-capex scare would hit it hard
PC demand cyclicality in CSG
watch: No earnings for ~52 days — clean runway
critical.valuation: Re-rated on the AI-server story but still reasonable vs pure-play AI names; TradingView STRONG BUY confirms.

analyses.name: MetLife, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A fresh-high VCP breakout with RS at the highs and RSI only 62 — an insurer leveraged to higher-for-longer rates, in the leading financials group. TradingView BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: MetLife is a diversified global life insurer and employee-benefits leader. Its earnings are driven by underwriting margins in group benefits, spread income on a large investment portfolio (which earns more as rates stay elevated), and fee income from asset management (MetLife Investment Management). Consistent buybacks and a solid dividend anchor total return.
highlights: Group Benefits a stable, market-leading franchise
Investment spread income benefits from higher-for-longer rates
MetLife Investment Management adds fee income
Strong capital position funding buybacks + dividend
Book-value and adjusted-EPS growth the key tells

accomplishments: Leading US group-benefits franchise
Consistent capital return through buybacks and dividend growth
Diversified international footprint (Asia, Latin America)

journey_52w.summary: A steady advance to fresh 52-week highs over the past year, breaking out of a 4-contraction VCP with RS at the highs as cool inflation and strong bank earnings powered the financials group in the latest session.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: Higher rates lift investment spread income

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Tightens into a VCP near highs

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: New-high breakout, RS at highs, RSI 62 — un-extended leadership

risks: Earnings ~23 days out
A sharp equity pullback pressures AUM/variable marks
A rapid rate-cut cycle would compress spread income
critical.valuation: Trades at a modest earnings multiple typical of life insurers; TradingView BUY corroborates the technical strength.

analyses.name: CVS Health Corporation
analyses.verdict: A new-high breakout on a +38% three-month turnaround with RS at the highs — a defensive-healthcare re-rating with real momentum. TradingView BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: CVS Health is an integrated healthcare company spanning retail pharmacy, a large pharmacy-benefit manager (Caremark), and health insurance (Aetna). The medical-loss ratio at Aetna and PBM economics are the swing factors; the turnaround thesis rests on margin recovery in the insurance segment and stable cash generation from pharmacy/PBM, supporting a substantial dividend.
highlights: Revenue among the largest in US healthcare (~$370B+ scale)
Aetna medical-loss ratio the key margin swing factor
Caremark PBM a large, cash-generative franchise
Turnaround narrative centered on insurance-margin recovery
Substantial dividend supported by cash flow

accomplishments: Integrated payer-provider-pharmacy model at national scale
Progress on Aetna margin stabilization driving the re-rating
Expanded health-services footprint

journey_52w.summary: From a beaten-down base a year ago, CVS staged a +38% three-month recovery to fresh 52-week highs as evidence of insurance-margin stabilization built — a defensive-healthcare re-rating with RS now at the highs.

milestones.label: Early 2026
milestones.note: Signs of Aetna medical-cost stabilization emerge

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Momentum builds; stock re-rates higher

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to a new 52-week high, RSI 65, RS at highs

risks: PBM/regulatory headline sensitivity
Aetna medical-cost surprises could stall the turnaround
Earnings ~22 days out
critical.valuation: Still inexpensive vs healthcare peers on normalized earnings; the re-rating has room if margins hold. TradingView BUY confirms.

analyses.name: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
analyses.verdict: At 52-week highs with RS at the highs and TradingView STRONG BUY — but earnings are imminent, so watch the print rather than chasing.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Goldman Sachs earns across Global Banking & Markets (investment banking advisory/underwriting + FICC and equities trading) and Asset & Wealth Management. Trading and IB revenues are the volatile swing factors and benefit from the risk-on tape; AWM adds more durable fee income. Book-value growth, ROE, and capital return via buybacks/dividend are the through-cycle scorecards.
highlights: Global Banking & Markets the largest revenue engine
Investment-banking backlog rebuilding with risk-on conditions
Asset & Wealth Management adding durable fee income
Strong capital ratios funding buybacks + dividend
ROE and book-value-per-share the key tells

accomplishments: Premier global M&A advisory and underwriting franchise
Growing, more-durable asset & wealth-management fee base
Consistent capital return and book-value compounding

journey_52w.summary: A strong year to fresh 52-week highs, with RS at the highs, as trading stayed active and the IB pipeline rebuilt. The stock ran ~26% over three months into an earnings report that lands essentially now — the reason it's a WATCH rather than a fresh entry.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: Trading revenues resilient; IB backlog rebuilds

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Risk-on tape lifts capital-markets activity

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: At new highs into earnings — event risk front and center

risks: EARNINGS IMMINENT (~0 days) — event risk; sell-the-news common after a big run
Trading/IB revenues are inherently volatile
A risk-off turn would hit capital-markets activity
watch: The earnings reaction — let it report before adding
critical.valuation: Around ~1.5-2x book depending on ROE; TradingView STRONG BUY reflects the technical strength, but the calendar argues for patience.

analyses.name: NVIDIA Corporation
analyses.verdict: Stabilizing 10% off its high on positive China headlines, RSI 61, basing not extended — the AI bellwether. TradingView BUY. Watch for the base to complete.

financials.summary: NVIDIA dominates AI accelerators via its data-center GPU franchise (the largest and fastest-growing segment), complemented by networking (post-Mellanox), gaming, and automotive. Data-center revenue and gross margin are the swing factors; the company generates enormous free cash flow funding buybacks. The key debate is the durability and pace of hyperscaler AI capex.
highlights: Data-center GPU revenue the dominant growth driver
Gross margins in the ~70%+ range at scale
Networking (InfiniBand/Ethernet) a fast-growing adjacency
Massive free cash flow funding buybacks
Hyperscaler AI-capex trajectory the central variable

accomplishments: Defined the AI-accelerator category and captured dominant share
Built a full-stack moat (CUDA software + GPU + networking)
Scaled data-center revenue to the largest segment in short order

journey_52w.summary: After a huge multi-year run, NVDA spent recent weeks consolidating — it pulled back ~10% from its high amid the semi-complex wobble on Middle East tensions, then firmed into the latest close on positive China headlines. RSI 61 and a VCP base suggest digestion rather than distribution.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: Data-center demand stays torrid; new-architecture ramp

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Semi complex wobbles on geopolitical risk; NVDA pulls back ~10%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Firms on positive China headlines; bases with RSI 61

risks: 10% off high — base not yet complete; wait for a reclaim of prior highs
China-export-policy headlines whipsaw both ways
Any sign of hyperscaler AI-capex digestion
critical.valuation: Rich on absolute terms but reasonable vs growth; TradingView BUY (16 buy / 1 sell). Earnings are ~44 days out, so the near-term driver is the tape and China headlines.

analyses.name: Dominion Energy, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A fresh-high VCP breakout with RS at the highs — a regulated utility catching both the defensive bid and the data-center power theme. TradingView STRONG BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Dominion is a regulated electric utility serving Virginia and the Carolinas, with Virginia's data-center corridor a structural load-growth driver. Rate-base growth and constructive regulatory outcomes drive earnings; the balance sheet has been de-levered and simplified after asset sales, and a well-covered dividend anchors total return. Offshore-wind project execution (CVOW) is a key capex and risk item.
highlights: Regulated Virginia/Carolinas franchise with data-center load growth
Rate-base growth the core earnings driver
Balance sheet simplified/de-levered post asset sales
Well-covered dividend after the reset
Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind (CVOW) the key capex project

accomplishments: Positioned at the center of Virginia's data-center power demand
Completed portfolio simplification and balance-sheet repair
Advanced the CVOW offshore-wind build

journey_52w.summary: A steady re-rating to fresh 52-week highs over the past year as the data-center load-growth story took hold and the balance-sheet repair reassured investors. The stock broke out of a tight VCP with RS at the highs into the latest session.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: Data-center demand narrative strengthens; balance-sheet repair credited

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Tightens into a VCP base near highs

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: New-high breakout, RS at highs, RSI 68

risks: Rate-sensitive — a back-up in yields would pressure it
Offshore-wind (CVOW) cost/schedule execution
RSI 68 warming; earnings ~17 days out
catalysts: Data-center load growth and associated rate-base
critical.valuation: Trades around the regulated-utility average on P/E and yield; the data-center angle adds a growth kicker. TradingView STRONG BUY confirms.
